summary: install.sh currently lacks --version/-V support. Both flags produce "error: unknown option" with exit code 1. The feature requires a short-circuit handler in argument parsing that prints a version string and exits 0 without any side effects, plus a usage text update.
evidence: grep -n 'version\|VERSION\|--version\|-V' install.sh returns no matches for version-related code
shellcheck install.sh .agent/scripts/*.sh passes with no warnings (baseline clean)
No git tags exist (git tag returns empty); version must be self-contained in install.sh
usage() function (lines 15-41) documents all options but contains no --version/-V entry
root_cause_or_rationale: install.sh was developed incrementally without a version-reporting feature. The issue requests it both as a useful installer capability and to validate the agent cycle's ability to pick up untagged issues.
implementation_plan: In install.sh line 2 (after `set -euo pipefail`), add a VERSION variable: `VERSION="1.0.0"`
In the argument-parsing `while (($#)); do case "$1" in` block (line 272), add as the FIRST case entry before `--engine-repository)`:
In the `usage()` function (around line 22, before the -h/--help line), add:
validation_plan: bash install.sh --version (expect: prints version string, exit 0, no files created/modified/deleted)
bash install.sh -V (expect: same output and exit code as --version)
diff <(ls -AR before) <(ls -AR after) after --version run (expect no diff)
bash .agent/scripts/validate.sh (expect exit 0 and all tests pass)
risks: The issue does not specify an exact version string; the recommended VERSION="1.0.0" may need maintainer adjustment to match the project's versioning scheme.
